owners.reason: TypeScript-only compiler storage and runtime support implements exact upstream Go behavior without claiming an upstream declaration identity.
The target-neutral TSTS embedding and session API is a deliberate public layer outside the upstream TS-Go command implementation.
The target-neutral TSTS extension and provider contracts are deliberate public APIs outside upstream TS-Go declarations.
Authored TypeScript runtime storage and private helpers implement reviewed Go standard-library or dependency facade semantics.
The TypeScript host command adapter is deliberate entrypoint infrastructure outside the upstream Go declaration surface.
The package-root export surface deliberately exposes reviewed compiler, embedding, and extension contracts beyond upstream Go declaration ownership.
